name: Talegones River Canyon Trail
name_original: Cañón del río Talegones – ida y vuelta de Lumías
description: Hike the moderate 8.5-mile Cañón del río Talegones round trip from Lumías, featuring river crossings and canyon scenery.
region_intro: Hiking around Berlanga De Duero offers routes through a landscape characterized by river canyons, historical sites, and gentle hills. The region features paths that traverse open countryside and lead to ancient castles and hermitages. Trails often follow river courses, providing varied terrain for exploration. Best hiking trails around Berlanga De Duero The most popular hiking route is Talegones River Canyon Trail, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural features of the river canyon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gormaz Castle Trail, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.3 km) path. This route leads to the historic Gormaz Castle, offering views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Mirador Portillo de Andaluz y Refugio Palomar Risco – circular desde Andaluz, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Berlanga De Duero is defined by river valleys, historical landmarks, and open countryside. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Berlanga De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Berlanga De Duero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Berlanga De Duero?
A: There are 6 hiking trails around Berlanga De Duero, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more moderate excursions through the region's unique landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Berlanga De Duero?
A: Yes, Berlanga De Duero offers 2 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or families. One option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059254497/ermita-de-san-baudelio-round-trip-from-casillas-de-berlanga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Baudelio – round trip from Casillas de Berlanga&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 3 km. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle – Berlanga de Duero loop from Berlanga de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, a 5 km loop that explores the local castle.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Berlanga De Duero?
A: Yes, several trails in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063946620/mirador-portillo-de-andaluz-y-refugio-palomar-risco-circular-desde-andaluz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Portillo de Andaluz y Refugio Palomar Risco – circular desde Andaluz&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle – Berlanga de Duero loop from Berlanga de Duero&lt;/a&gt; is also a circular route.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The trails often traverse river canyons and open countryside. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6498473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caracena River Canyon&lt;/a&gt; and discover natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7170242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabina La Pica&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2549872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Stone Arch in the Caracena Canyon&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062321130/talegones-river-canyon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talegones River Canyon Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the natural features of a river canyon.

Q: Are there historical landmarks to explore while hiking in Berlanga De Duero?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can hike to impressive sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gormaz Castle&lt;/a&gt;, one of the largest Islamic fortresses in Europe, or the local &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berlanga Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059099105/gormaz-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gormaz Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated route to this historic landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Berlanga De Duero?
A: The best time for hiking in Berlanga De Duero is generally during spring and autumn when the temperatures are mild and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winters can be chilly but offer a different, quieter experience.

Q: Are there any trails with scenic viewpoints in the area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063946620/mirador-portillo-de-andaluz-y-refugio-palomar-risco-circular-desde-andaluz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Portillo de Andaluz y Refugio Palomar Risco – circular desde Andaluz&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for leading through scenic viewpoints, offering expansive views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Is parking generally available at trailheads in Berlanga De Duero?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't always detailed, most rural trailheads in the Berlanga De Duero area typically have informal parking spaces or roadside pull-offs available. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Berlanga De Duero?
A: The trails in Berlanga De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers often praise the region's blend of natural beauty, historical sites, and the peaceful, open countryside.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Berlanga De Duero?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Berlanga De Duero region, especially in open countryside areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

Q: Are there any trails that follow river canyons?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by river canyons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062321130/talegones-river-canyon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talegones River Canyon Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a route that explores the natural features of a river canyon.
tips: In the beautiful town of Lumías, we find this beautiful waterfall.

The Talegones River Canyon Bridge, although not a monumental structure like a traditional Roman or medieval bridge, is part of the nature and hiking trail that crosses this spectacular spot in the municipality of Berlanga de Duero, province of Soria.
At this point we find a narrow stone bridge that helps us cross the Talegones River as it passes through the town of Torrevicente.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cañón del río Talegones trail from Lumías?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the village of Lumías itself, which serves as the starting point for this round trip. Look for designated parking areas or suitable spots within the village before heading to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Cañón del río Talegones trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the frequent river crossings and the presence of wildlife, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring is highly recommended, especially for witnessing the spectacular bloom of wild narcissus. It's also a good idea to check the river's water level, as some sections might be dry during the summer months.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to hike the Cañón del río Talegones trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I know about the river crossings on this route?
faqs.answer: A distinctive feature of this route is the multitude of river crossings, reportedly up to 19 times on the way up and 17 on the way back. These often involve improvised steps of large stones or small footbridges. While most are excellent, some might require careful footing, so wear appropriate waterproof footwear.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cañón del río Talegones trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows the Talegones river through a limestone canyon. The path meanders alongside the water, often shaded by diverse riverside flora like ash, poplar, and willow trees. You'll encounter escarpments typical of karst relief and impressive rock formations. The path is generally considered easy to simple.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered easy to simple, making it accessible for most hikers, including families. The frequent river crossings can be a fun and engaging element for children, but ensure they have appropriate footwear and supervision.

faqs.question: What natural features or points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse a beautiful limestone canyon carved by the Talegones river. Notable features include the Lumías Waterfall, the Talegones Canyon Bridge, and the scenic Talegones River itself. The canyon also features numerous rocky shelters and lush riparian vegetation.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The canyon is rich in biodiversity. You might spot rupicolous birds and raptors such as griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, peregrine falcons, and golden eagles. Deer and wild boars can also be found in the area, especially in quieter moments.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other long-distance routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route are well-marked as part of the GR-86, the Iberian Soriano Trail. It also briefly intersects with parts of the Camino del Cid (Senderista) and the Camino del Cid.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Both Lumías and the nearby village of Torrevicente are small, charming villages. While amenities might be limited, you may find local establishments offering food or accommodation. It's advisable to check in advance, especially during off-peak seasons.
